PUBLIC REPORT OF LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN AND 6-7 TREES SNAPPED OFF OR UPROOTED. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

*** 3 INJ *** POSSIBLE DAMAGE FROM A TORNADO. KISSIMMEE PRAIRIE PRESERVE STATE PARK BIOLOGIST REPORTS A FEW FLIPPED CAMPERS, SOME BIG TREES UPROOTED, SHEET METAL LODGED IN A TREE. INJURIES WERE FROM PEOPLE INSIDE CAMPERS WHEN THEY FLIPPED. DAMAGE REPORTED IS CENTRALIZED IN THE PARK FROM THE EQUESTRIAN CAMPGROUND TO FAMILY CAMPGROUND. PARK BIOLOGIST REPORTS DAMAGE OCCURRED FROM 930-940AM, WHICH MATCHES RADAR.
PRIVATE CITIZEN REPORTS DOWNED TREES ON THE ROAD AT THE INTERSECTION OF US-441 AND NW 353TH STREET NEAR FT. DRUM.

POTENTIAL TORNADO DAMAGE. PER FDEM STATE WATCH OFFICE...OKEECHOBEE SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTS DAMAGE TO HOMES NORTH OF FT. DRUM...APPROXIMATE LOCATION IS 1335 NE 342ND TRAIL. NO WORD ON EXTENT OF DAMAGE. TIMING OF DAMAGE IS APPROXIMATE USING RADAR.

A SMALL BUT INTENSE THUNDERSTORM PRODUCED SPORADIC WIND DAMAGE OVER NORTHWEST ORANGE COUNTY. SEVERAL TREES AND LARGE BRANCHES WERE REPORTED DOWNED BY WEKIWA SPRINGS STATE PARK OFFICIALS. MINOR FENCE DAMAGE AND A LARGE PART OF A TREE BROKE OFF IN THE BACKYARD OF A HOME NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF SINGING PALM AND SWAYING PALM DRIVES IN APOPKA. A LARGE TREE SNAPPED AND FELL ON AN UNOCCUPIED CAR IN THE PARKING LOT OF A FURNITURE STORE IN NEARBY ALTAMONTE SPRINGS ON SR-436.
MESONET SITE MEASURED PEAK WIND GUST OF 34 KT FROM THE SOUTHWEST.

AN INTENSE THUNDERSTORM MOVED NORTHEAST ALONG I-4 IN CENTRAL ORANGE COUNTY AND PRODUCED SEVERAL REPORTS OF MINOR WIND DAMAGE IN AND AROUND THE CITY OF ORLANDO. A GAS STATION SIGN WAS DAMAGED NEAR THE CORNER OF I-4 AND JOHN YOUNG PARKWAY. A LARGE TREE BRANCH FELL ON TWO PARKED CARS AT A HOTEL NEXT TO I-4 IN ALTAMONTE SPRINGS. A LARGE TREE TOPPLED IN THE FRONT YARD OF A HOME OFF GOLDENROAD ROAD IN WINTER PARK. A LARGE TREE BRANCH FELL ON AN EMPTY VEHICLE PARKED ON CONCORD ST. IN DOWNTOWN ORLANDO.

PRELIMINARY TORNADO. 6 TRAILERS DESTROYED ON INDIAN RIVER DRIVE IN PALM BAY. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

UPDATED EARLIER REPORT. BASED ON DAMAGE PHOTOS AND VIDEO ON WORTH COURT IN PALM BAY, NWS CONFIRMS DAMAGE WAS CAUSED BY A TORNADO OF AT LEAST EF0 STRENGTH - AT LEAST 65-85 MPH. TORNADO BEGAN AS A WATERSPOUT OVER THE INDIAN RIVER, MOVED ONSHORE, AND DAMAGED HOMES ON WORTH COURT, CROSSED US-1 AND CAUSED DAMAGE TO 6 MANUFACTURED HOMES ON INDIAN RIVER DRIVE NE.
